read: Spot is coiling just below the dominant 740 Call/Major Wall (GEX 2.2B, largest on board) in a positive-gamma pin regime where charm into the final hour gravitates price back toward 740 — fade the next clean re-test of 740 with a 1-pt stop, that is the desk's primary order.

levels.why: Dominant GEX wall (2212M, +515 building) with 110k OI and highest theta node (7.8); pending/unclean today's touch de-rates from 70%+ but structural mass is unmatched — every other resistance level is a footnote to this one.

Second-tier Call Wall with GEX 675M building and the highest |charm| of any resistance strike (-59K vs 740's -12K); the primary conditional fade if 740 breaks — charm magnitude uniquely distinguishes it from 743.

Largest-OI cluster below spot (81k) with the second-biggest GEX on the downside (481M) and a positive charm floor; the key catch if 737.5 zero-gamma loses — structurally 9x stronger than 730 on GEX alone.

Gamma-flip tripwire where dealer behavior shifts from stabilizing to amplifying; confirmed by today's earlier clean resistance hold from below — a functional regime-change level, not an OI-mass level, and that is what separates it from 735.

Third Call Wall with the dataset's largest |charm| (-114K) and 77k OI, but 10+ points OTM — high-conviction backstop only if 745 breaks; charm magnitude alone keeps it on the board despite the distance.
Largest combined OI anchor on the downside (100k) makes it a structural backstop, but GEX is modest (51M vs 735's 481M) and 9+ point distance limits near-term relevance.
